chapters.title: Role models can simultaneously inspire and frighten
chapters.text:
Will Smith was born... No, let's start with something else.

The actor remembers his childhood through two strong emotions — delight and fear. They were the most common responses to his father's actions. The children called him Daddio.

Born in Philadelphia and considered a troubled child, Daddio was brilliant and brave. Having escaped from the boarding school where his parents sent him to study, he voluntarily joined the United States Air Force.

Daddio also had an entrepreneurial flair, which allowed him to start his business several times from scratch. But all his life, he was haunted by two enemies — alcoholism and anger. Therefore, his family always admired his knowledge and willingness to act but were afraid to do something against his will.

The only one who dared to point out his father's faults and mistakes was his wife and Will's mother, Carolyn Elaine Bright. An educated and independent woman, she fell in love with the intelligence and charisma of this man. But over time, she noticed that his ideas were replaced by a desire to control his workers and family members. Daddio couldn't stand it when she made remarks to him, and quarrels could lead to physical violence.

Some men believe there can't be two “commanders” in the family as it may lead to drama.

Therefore, Smith’s childhood, which from the outside might seem ideal, hid many tragic memories. Considering his parents as role models, he didn't know what to do when they couldn't get along.

Did you know? Will's father named his son after himself — Willard Carroll Smith II — and didn't like it when people called him Will Jr., believing it humiliated them both.

chapters.title: Imagination makes events more interesting
chapters.text:
The experience of conflict situations in the family and his relationships with peers developed Smith's empathy. He learned to recognize different emotions and mood swings easily. Moreover, he could accurately predict how this or that action would affect a person's well-being.

These skills were one of the main reasons Smith succeeded in his career. He quickly got used to the role and knew how a person feels when:

• Faced with a difficult choice
• Someone meets the love of their life
• A person wants to seem cool

He also built a great relationship with his fans and listeners, making people feel like best friends with their idol.

Childhood also gave him a vivid imagination. Hiding from real problems in fictional worlds, he developed a unique taste and perspective. He wasn't interested in the toys and games that other children liked. And also, if he found meaning in unexpected pursuits and things, the opinion of others didn't bother him much.

For example, he went through a phase of admiring cowboy boots. And if trendsetters these days agree with his taste, in the 70s, friends and neighbors ridiculed it. But he continued to wear pair after pair because he felt confident and cool in his boots.

Also, along with Carolyn, Will invented a game. Returning home from summer camp or school, he told her about everything he had experienced, with a few fictional additions, and his mother had to guess which were true.

Creative habits in childhood help solve problems in adulthood.

A family trip to the Grand Canyon also influenced Will’s imagination. It was the only year that Daddio allowed himself to take a vacation, and they went to see all their relatives, stopping at a national park on the way. The canyons captivated Will with their grandeur and reminded him that anything can exist in the world, no matter how fantastic it may seem.

Did you know? As a child, Will Smith had an imaginary friend, Magicer. Unlike other children who used such characters to describe their feelings and validate their opinions, Magicker's point of view could sometimes differ from Will's. Appreciating her son's feelings, his mother put out an extra plate for his imaginary friend during dinner.

chapters.title: A family split and search for consolation
chapters.text:
While Daddio was the epitome of discipline for Will, his mother was an example of education. But it was Gigi, his grandmother, who taught him to feel love, gratitude, and to see the presence of God in everything.

Once, he witnessed how his grandmother invited a homeless woman into her home, fed her, helped her take a bath, and chose things from her wardrobe. He was struck by the realization that Gigi was doing this from the bottom of her heart, not trying to please someone or wanting to earn “extra points” as a Christian. His grandmother's love for life, passion, and desire to do good deeds impressed Will.

And it was Gigi who inspired him to make his first public performance. After playing the piano during a Sunday service, Will fell in love with the audience’s reaction and decided to dedicate his life to inspiring others.

After that, he took any opportunity to perform on stage, recreate a movie scene with friends, or amuse the family with his antics. And when Daddio bought a camera (an incredible luxury at the time), there were many shots of Will dancing, singing, or acting on birthdays and trips.

Such moments were among the few heartwarming moments in the Smith family, where misunderstandings increased yearly.

At thirteen, Will saw his father beating his mother. After that, she took her things and started living with Gigi. She was followed by Will's younger brother, Harry. All family members continued to keep in touch, but their usual comfort and sense of security disappeared.

Daddio began spending more time with the children, becoming the sole commander of their family. Will's cousin Paul, who lacked discipline, also moved in with them. A sharp boy who constantly found himself in trouble, miraculously got along with everyone around him. In particular, he found the key to Will's heart through music.

In the 70s, New York was the epicenter of most musical styles, including hip-hop.

Will discovered the uniqueness of hip-hop culture for himself. He was impressed by how beats and words can affect people, change their moods, and help them forget about everything around them.

chapters.title: DJ Jazzy Jeff & The Fresh Prince start a music career
chapters.text:
The young performer found himself writing rap lyrics and arranging song battles at school and on the streets, coining the name The Fresh Prince. Among his strengths were charisma, humor, and the absence of profanity. Sometimes, he communicated in rhymes even with his teachers, who were amused by his enthusiasm. They noticed not only his talent for studying but also for performing.

Comedy is an extension of intelligence. It's hard to be really funny if you're not really smart. ~ Will Smith

As in his daily duties, Will was disciplined in writing his lyrics. He constantly wrote down his ideas, joined street battles, played at parties, and developed his skills. He didn't stay long where rap was considered just entertainment because rehearsals and performances became real work for him.

That is why he considers meeting with the performer Jazzy Jeff a gift from heaven. Having survived a serious illness and being constantly under the supervision of his mother, Jazzy Jeff found his vocation in music. In the basement of his house, he made a studio where he spent whole days mixing songs and experimenting with various musical instruments.

At one of the parties, Will played with him. No one present had ever heard such beats combined with meaningful and funny lyrics. It was the first performance of the duo DJ Jazzy Jeff & The Fresh Prince, who, in a short time, went on to conquer New York at the New Music Seminar festival.

In the ‘70s and ‘80s, the new artists’ fame popularized their hometowns.

After receiving the title of the King of the DJ world in ‘86, Jazzy Jeff became even more active in performing at events and writing music. Together with Will, they even released their first record. Unfortunately, their partners were from a little-known music company, so it didn't give them recognition or big sales.

Wanting to focus on music, Will didn't plan to enter college, which became a constant cause of quarrels with Carolyn. Daddio came to his aid. The parents agreed to give their son a year to develop a musical career and promised to support him. And if he failed, he would go to college.

Shortly after this conversation, The Fresh Prince heard his song on the radio.

chapters.title: Professional successes and personal dramas
chapters.text:
As soon as Will finished school, they went on tour. The youngest of their team, Omar, was sixteen, so homework and lessons hadn't completely disappeared from Will's life. Sometimes, instead of evening entertainment and rest, they all sat together and did homework because they promised Omar’s teachers that the tour wouldn't prevent him from graduating.

However, they faced many adult problems. They had to deal with financial issues, the laws of different states, and the organization of performances. The need to pay bills, conflicts with the police, theft of belongings, and many other nuances that made them understand the music world didn't consist only of recording albums and applauses.

Will and Jazzy Jeff also realized that the contract with their friend Dana Goodman and his small record company slowed their development. Therefore, they signed a contract with London-based Jive, which promised them access to the international market. They kept their word: after the tour, the duo went to London to record their second album.

The 31st Grammy ceremony was the first to feature a rap category. Jazzy Jeff & The Fresh Prince were nominated.

Will's private life was the opposite of his success on stage. In the early years of his performances, his love for his first girlfriend, Melanie Parker, charged and inspired him. He sincerely wanted to start a family with her, so he saw every concert not as an opportunity to have fun but as a chance to earn money for their future. But after returning from tour, Will had a terrible realization: Melanie had cheated on him.

His world was shattered. Having lost trust in a loved one, he sought solace in expensive purchases and short romances. The singer bought a big house, which became a place of constant and loud parties.

He tried to reunite with Melanie, but he couldn't forgive her, no matter how hard he tried to hide it. The drama of his private life affected his career negatively. After flying to the Bahamas for six weeks to record a new album, the crew spent the entire time partying with the girls and losing money. Will was taken from there by his father, who quickly brought him back to his senses with his disappointment.

chapters.title: Be ready for a second chance
chapters.text:
Challenges came one after another. Their next album failed, and the duo was no longer asked to appear on TV shows or invited to concerts. Will Smith didn't pay his taxes on time, so he was forced to sell his cars, motorcycles, and house. Moreover, his regular parties in dubious company led to police accusations of connections with gangsters.

Will had to move from Philly to Los Angeles, and he realized how many terrible mistakes he had made. It was the wrong move to try to heal a broken heart with his popularity. He didn't manage to save either his relationships or his career.

It’s respectable to lose to the universe. It’s a tragedy to lose to yourself. ~ Will Smith

But the universe decided to give him a second chance. Invited to Quincy Jones's birthday party, Will heard from the birthday boy that he could get the lead role in a new TV series. He asked for time to prepare for the audition, but Quincy listed how much could change in the coming days, and he may lose the opportunity. That is why the audition for The Fresh Prince of Bel-Air took place at the same party with hundreds of Hollywood guests. The director signed the contract with Will the same evening.

The universe has a lot of possibilities for you. You must react to them, even when you’re not ready.

During filming, Will also negotiated a supporting role for Jazzy Jeff. And although, at first, he didn't want to move to Los Angeles, over time, he began to enjoy working in television. Spectators who came to live recordings of Friday episodes were always in for a pleasant surprise — DJ Jazzy Jeff & The Fresh Prince entertained them during breaks.

As a new, stable source of income, television allowed them to work on the new album without feeling pressure. The result was fantastic: they wrote twice as many songs in less time than the last. Their album Homebase won an American Music Award, and the song Summertime, written by The Fresh Prince, won a Grammy. Life was getting better again.

chapters.title: Acting success lies in profitable films
chapters.text:
Previous experience had taught Will how to plan. After all, everything ends, no matter how stable it seems. In his life, there were already music performances and television. What else could he want?

An honest answer to this question scared him. He realized that he would like to become a big movie star. But to the question “How?” he had no answer. Fortunately, his agent, Jay, came to his rescue.

They started by identifying the images and roles Will would like to try. They agreed that his characters should be brave, funny, and romantic at the same time. With these principles in mind, they began using every live shooting of an episode of The Fresh Prince of Bel-Air as a platform to connect with producers, directors, and actors.

To avoid a crisis, ask yourself what you want from life and how to achieve it.

Their work quickly paid off. Will received an offer to play a minor role in a film about gangsters with a salary of ten million dollars. He was overjoyed but refused the offer. After discussing the script with Jay, he realized that his character didn't correspond to the image he wanted to create for himself.

This offer was quickly replaced by another. He was invited to play a complex and deep character in Six Degrees of Separation. The salary for this project was ten times lower, but the film's significant influence and positive impression on the audience were worth much more.

But history, unfortunately, repeated itself. After receiving such a desirable first role and hearing favorable reviews from film critics, he suffered from problems in his personal life.

Having met Sheree Zampino while filming and falling in love with her, Will soon proposed. They had a son, Trey, and the young actor swore to God that he would be the best father for him.

But the intense filming, the dissolution of Will into his characters, and his idealized vision of Sheree destroyed their marriage. Will was once again left alone with thoughts about whether true love existed and whether he could ever build a perfect relationship.

chapters.title: Success brings extra responsibility
chapters.text:
During a difficult period in Will’s life, Jada Pinkett appeared. He saw her the same day he met Sheree Zampino and felt an uncontrollable attraction, but they only talked for the first time when Will was going through the divorce. Their long, deep conversations, mutual sexual interest, and vision of a future together indicated they were made for each other. Joy returned to Will Smith's life again.

Even when they married and had a son, the actor didn't forget about his first family. To not sever the bond with Trey, he and Jada even encouraged him to come up with a name for his little brother. A fan of computer games, he suggested Luigi. The shocked couple had to join this “game” to change Trey's mind. As a result, their child received a triple name — Jaden Christopher Syre. You can try to guess who suggested which name.

At the same time, Will Smith added Bad Boys and Independence Day to his filmography. Scenes with the actor spectacularly catching up with a car or defeating aliens have become world-famous.

Movie stars receive fan loyalty when they travel to movie premieres.

However, the real awareness of his professionalism and influence came when he was offered the leading role in a film about Muhammad Ali. Feeling an insane responsibility, he refused at first. But a private meeting with Muhammad Ali and his family changed everything. Smith felt grateful for the trust and willingness to take on this project.

Smith had been training with professional boxers, completely changing his regime, eating style, and even behavior. His trainer, Darrell, was preparing him for the new film and the next phase of his life. He taught the actor and his team to act as a whole, find their true vocation, and strive for love and understanding in this world.

Did you know? At first, Will Smith turned down the role in Men in Black because he worried another movie about aliens would fail. But he changed his mind after meeting Steven Spielberg, who invited the actor to his house and told him about special techniques in his film.

chapters.title: Conclusion
chapters.text:
The life of celebrities seems to be cloudless, easy, and happy. But Will Smith's story shows how many challenges they must overcome to become who they are. Even when he thought he had reached all possible heights, new challenges and avenues for growth appeared. Nevertheless, his career is still a role model and inspiration for many. So how can you become the “Will Smith” in your life?

First of all, be ready to make your family your greatest asset. Achieve goals, increase financial wealth, and develop to improve the well-being of the people you care about. Listen to your family members when they share their feelings. Because sometimes, you can miss a critical moment when your partner tells you they can't live in such a big house or your child doesn't want to perform on stage anymore.

Secondly, don't become a greedy collector of achievements. Listen to your inner voice, which can tell you when you should stop, rest, and find a new vector for development.

Act out of love for this world and the desire to increase the number of good deeds in it. Remember that the universe is a very mobile and sensitive material that reacts to changes and takes the side of people with positive actions. Listen to your intuition and move forward.

Of course, this guide is not a solution to every potential problem you may encounter. But these rules worked for the cowboy boots guy, who became Hollywood's highest-paid actor. Maybe they will work for you too.

Try this



• Listen to DJ Jazzy Jeff & The Fresh Prince's albums and see if events from his biography are reflected in their songs.
• Regularly reflect on who you are and who you want to become so that you do not stray from the chosen path.

Read it till the end?
